topic: Mastra vs Agno vs Strands 2026: TypeScript vs Python AI Agent Framework Compared

key_points: Mastra focuses on TypeScript-first development with agents, workflows, and RAG primitives

stats.fact: Mastra reached 22,000+ GitHub stars and 300,000+ weekly npm downloads by its 1.0 release in January 2026
Mastra raised a $22M Series A led by Spark Capital in April 2026, bringing total funding to $35M
Mastra is used in production by PayPal, Adobe, and Replit
Mastra's Model Router indexes 3,300+ models from 94 providers with automatic fallback
Mastra cut development time from 41 hours (LangChain) to 18 hours, and improved task completion rate from 87.4% to 94.2%
Agno (formerly Phidata, rebranded January 2025) has 39,000+ GitHub stars with 400+ contributors
Agno agents instantiate in ~2μs on average — 10,000x faster than LangGraph — using only 3.75KiB of memory (50x less than LangGraph)
Agno provides 100+ pre-built tool integrations including web search, data analysis, file ops, and MCP servers
Strands Agents SDK reached 14 million+ downloads since its May 2025 open source launch
Strands Agents reached 1 million downloads on GitHub within weeks of launch
Strands Agents 1.0 introduced multi-agent patterns (Graph, Swarm, Workflow) and A2A protocol for cross-framework interoperability
TypeScript surged 66% in 2026 developer activity, threatening Python's AI dominance according to GitHub Octoverse
Strands is backed by Accenture, Anthropic, Langfuse, mem0.ai, Meta, PwC, Ragas.io, and Tavily

angles: TypeScript is finally a first-class citizen for AI agents — Mastra proves it with $35M total funding and Fortune 500 adoption
Agno's 10,000x speed advantage over LangGraph reframes the performance conversation: milliseconds matter at agent scale
Strands is the 'escape hatch' framework — AWS-backed but provider-agnostic, ideal for teams that want cloud optionality without lock-in
The Python monopoly on AI agents is breaking: 2026 is the year TypeScript teams stop apologizing for not using Python
Three frameworks, three philosophies: Mastra (full-stack TypeScript), Agno (raw Python performance), Strands (cloud-native observability)
Which framework wins the 'full-stack AI developer' war? Mastra integrates with Next.js/tRPC; Agno integrates with FastAPI; Strands integrates with AWS Lambda
MCP (Model Context Protocol) support comparison: all three frameworks now support MCP but with very different integration depths
Production war stories: PayPal/Adobe use Mastra, AWS-native companies use Strands — what does your infrastructure say about your framework choice?
